properties.source: 2 December News; Aden al Ghad; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Al Sahil Al Gharbi
properties.notes: On 13 November 2024, an unidentified gunman shot and killed 1 Houthi supervisor in Sanaa - Ar Rawdah (Bani Al Harith, Amanat al Asimah). The Houthi supervisor was in charge of one of the secret prisons, the gunman shot the leader as he was leaving the prison killing him and injuring 1 of his guards. The source claimed that the reason behind the incident was likely that the perpetrator was tortured while in prison by the Houthi supervisor.

properties.actor2: Police Forces of Yemen (2017-) Houthi - Prison Guards
properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Al Sahil Al Gharbi; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Yemen Window

Sources suggested that the assassination was in retaliation for the Houthi leader's alleged role in releasing suspects accused of killing the gunman's brother and manipulating the investigation. Sources also claimed that the perpetrator received help from the prison guards.

On 24 November 2024, Houthi militants assaulted a citizen (unspecified form of assault) in the Hazm Al Udayn district center (Ibb). The victim was attacked by five militants as he returned from collecting a money transfer.

properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Yemeni Network for Rights and Freedoms
properties.notes: On 18 March 2025, a member of the Houthi navy forces shot a male child in Al Luhayyah (Alluheyah, Al Hudaydah) resulting in his death. The Houthis claimed that the killing was done by mistake. 1 fatality.

properties.assoc_actor_2: Labor Group (Yemen)
properties.notes: On 18 March 2025, Houthi traffic police forces abducted tens of street vendors and small shop owners in Al Hudaydah (Al Hudaydah) and moved them to their prisons as part of a large campaign aimed at removing and destroying kiosks, stalls and small shops in an attempt to keep only Houthi businesses operating in the city. The campaign took place over 18 and 19 March 2025. Accordingly, it is split across 2 events.

properties.assoc_actor_2: Labor Group (Yemen)
properties.notes: On 19 March 2025, Houthi traffic police forces abducted tens of street vendors and small shop owners in Al Hudaydah (Al Hudaydah) and moved them to their prisons as part of a large campaign aimed at removing and destroying kiosks, stalls and small shops in an attempt to keep only Houthi businesses operating in the city. properties.assoc_actor_2: Labor Group (Yemen)
properties.notes: Around 21 March 2025 (as reported), a Houthi supervisor (Mushrif) raided a construction materials store and abducted 1 worker in an unspecified location in a Houthi-controlled area (coded to Sanaa) (Amanat al Asimah). The source speculated that the abduction was to pressure the shop owners into paying additional levies.

properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen)
properties.notes: On 17 March 2025, tribesmen from the Al Thamathima tribal militia shot and a Houthi supervisor in Al Thummim village in Wadi Khaywan (Harf Sufyan, Amran) causing his death. The reason for the killing was the fact that the supervisor had led a military campaign against the tribal militia a year ago causing the death of a sheikh from that tribe.

properties.assoc_actor_2: Al Thamathima Tribal Group (Yemen)
properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen)
properties.notes: Around 18 March 2025 (as reported), a large Houthi military campaign consisting of a number of military crews and vehicles as well as tens of armed soldiers was sent to Al Thamathima village in Wadi Khaywan region (Harf Sufyan, Amran). The Houthi forces then blockaded the village, seized the farms of the Al Thamathima tribes, destroyed their qat crops and stole solar lamps from the water wells. They also shot randomly at the houses of the residents. Casualties unknown. The reason for the campaign was the earlier killing of a Houthi supervisor in that same village by tribesmen of the Al Thamathima tribal militia.

properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 20 March 2025, Houthi soldiers opened fire on a car carrying a family in Aflah Ash Shawm district (coded to district center Aflah ash Sham) (Hajjah), killing 2 people, a father and his son. The reason behind the attack was not reported.

properties.assoc_actor_2: Labor Group (Yemen)
properties.source: 2 December News; Al Harf 28; Al Masdar; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 15 March 2025, Houthi members physically assaulted, kidnapped, tortured, and shot at 1 worker at a motorcycle repair workshop in Qahzah (Al Dhihar, Ibb). The reason behind the attack was verbal dispute between the mechanic and a Houthi members who then attacked and kidnapped the mechanic to an unknown destination where he was tortured and shot at. The Houthi members left the mechanic in critical condition on a main road where he was found by civilians and taken to the hospital.

properties.assoc_actor_2: Muslim Group (Yemen)
properties.source: Al Masdar; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Twitter; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 17 March 2025, Houthi forces abducted an imam in Dhawdan village (An Nadirah, Ibb). Houthi armed men raided the mosque after evening prayer and abducted the imam and took him to one of their prisons in the district. The reason behind the abduction was that the imam did not allow a Houthi-affiliated imam to deliver the previous Friday sermon in the village mosque.

properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 21 March 2025, Houthi soldiers shot and injured 1 fellow Houthi soldier in Al Qaidah (Dhi As Sufal, Ibb). The wounded soldier worked at the Public Works Office, and the sources claimed that the attackers were loyal to the head of the Environmental Health Department at the same office. The reason behind the clash was not reported, but one source claimed that it stemmed from disputes among local Houthi leaders over influence and financial resources. There were no fatalities.

On 16 March 2025, the Giants-affiliated Saba Axis clashed with Houthi forces using medium and heavy weapons and 'thwarted their infiltration attempt' in the Mala mountain region (Harib, Marib). Casualties unknown.

properties.actor2: Military Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council
properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Defense Liney; Khabar News Agency (Yemen)
properties.notes: On 17 March 2025, IRG forces exchanged shelling with and 'repelled' a Houthi attack on IRG forces on the northern front of Marib governorate (coded to Asdas) (Raghwan, Marib) and the western front of Marib (coded as Suq Sirwah) (Sirwah). The shelling and clashes resulted in 1 fatality and 4 injuries on the IRG side and the destruction of Houthi ammunitions, weapons and heavy equipment designed for constructing fortifications. IRG forces also intercepted a number of Houthi drones flying over the frontlines. Fatalities split across 2 events. 1 fatality coded to this location.

properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen)

The clashes resulted in unspecified Houthi fatalities and injuries and the destruction of Houthi military vehicles in addition to 1 fatality and 4 injuries on the IRG side. Unclear fatalities coded as 3 on the Houthi side. Total fatalities coded as 4.

properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Defense Liney; Khabar News Agency (Yemen)

properties.assoc_actor_2: Al Jalha Tribal Group (Yemen)
properties.source: 2 December News; Aden al Ghad; Al Harf 28; Al Masdar; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Al Sahil Al Gharbi; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 15 March 2025, Houthi forces abducted a number of residents in Jalha (Monabbih, Sadah). They were taken to Houthi district prison. The reason behind the abduction was not reported. The abduction occurred after Houthi forces raided and looted a house of a Jalha tribal sheikh. The forces raided the house, fired different weapons to scare the residents, and looted money, cars, jewelry, and belongings of the sheikh. Houthi forces claimed the reason behind the raid was to look for wanted persons who fled from the district prison.

properties.actor1: Military Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council - STC Shabwani Defense Forces
properties.source: Al Harf 28; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Twitter
properties.notes: On 15 March 2025, STC's Shabwani Defense Forces abducted 1 activist at the electricity checkpoint in Ataq (Ataq, Shabwah) and taken to an unknown location. The forces took the activist as he was passing through the checkpoint. The reason behind the abduction was claims that there was a report against him. Some sources claimed he was held in a water tank and later released, but one source claimed he was still abducted.

properties.assoc_actor_2: Farmers (Yemen)
properties.notes: On 7 November 2024, Houthi forces abducted a number of farmers in Saadah governorate (coded to governorate capital Sadah) (Sadah) after refusing to give up their lands to the Houthi forces as they rely on the lands' agricultural crops.

properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 12 December 2024, armed residents (likely tribal) clashed with Houthi forces in Wadi Dhaba region in Dhi As Sufal district (coded to district center Dhi As Sufal) (Ibb). The reason behind the clashes was the attempt of a Houthi investor to establish a crusher project in the area despite the rejection of the residents. The residents expelled the crusher and the equipment considering it a danger to their health and safety, in addition to threatening their agricultural and animal wealth. There were no casualties.

properties.actor1: Military Forces of the United States (2021-2025)
properties.source: Al Harf 28; Al Masdar; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Al Masirah; Ansar Allah; Yemen News Agency (SABA) - Houthi; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 5 January 2025, at an unspecified time, suspected US forces conducted 3 airstrikes targeting unspecified Houthi sites in Sadah governorate (coded to governorate capital Sadah). Casualties unknown. US forces did not claim the strikes.

On 9 January 2025, tribesmen clashed with Houthi forces at a checkpoint in Sadah governorate (coded to governorate capital Sadah) (Saadah) resulting in the killing of 1 Houthi field leader. The reason behind the clashes was due to the killing of 1 tribesman in one of the Houthi checkpoints.

Around 28 December 2024 (as reported), Houthi forces conducted an abduction campaign in Al Abdin and Ghuraz (Saadah, Sadah), targeting associates of Presidential Leadership Council member Othman Mujalli, alleging that they were part of a spy network led by Mujalli's brother in collaboration with Saudi and Western intelligence agencies. The number of people abducted was not reported. Coded as 2 separate events.

Around 28 December 2024 (as reported), Houthi forces conducted an abduction campaign in Al Abdin and Ghuraz (Saadah, Sadah), targeting associates of Presidential Leadership Council member Othman Mujalli, alleging that they were part of a spy network led by Mujalli's brother in collaboration with Saudi and Western intelligence agencies. The number of people abducted was not reported. Coded as 2 separate events.

properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 19 March 2025, Houthi forces abducted 1 of their activists in Amanat al Asimah governorate (coded to governorate capital Sanaa). The reason behind the abduction was not reported. He was taken to an unknown destination.

properties.source: 2 December News; Mareb Press; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 24 March 2025, Houthi forces abducted over 15 civilians from different areas of Amanat al Asimah governorate (coded to governorate capital Sanaa). The reason behind the abduction was that the civilians were using their phones in public for too long as they suspected that the civilians are collaborating with the US forces and sending coordinates for them to strike. They were taken to an unknown destination.

On 25 March 2025, Tribesmen from Khawlan tribe clashed with Houthi forces in Kawfal Camp (Sirwah, Marib) resulting in the killing of 7 in total from both sides. The reason behind the clashes was that the Houthi leader had raped a boy from Khawlan tribe.

properties.source: 2 December News; Al Harf 28; Al Masdar; Khabar News Agency (Yemen)
properties.notes: On 26 March 2025, Houthi forces abducted 1 manuscript researcher from his house in Zabid (Zabid, Al Hudaydah). The Houthi forces raided the house for the third time and damaged it. The reason behind the abduction was not reported. He were taken to an unknown destination.

properties.actor2: Military Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council - STC
properties.source: Al Harf 28; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Dera Al Ganoob; Hadramawt 21; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 23 March 2025, IRG Southern forces (coded as STC generic forces) clashed with Houthi forces that tried to advance to their locations in Habil Naji, Shiab Ahb, and As Sahilah in the Hajar area of Thawkab sector (Al Husha, Ad Dali). The clash resulted in 'heavy losses' among Houthis. Unclear casualties coded as 0 on the Houthi side following the most conservative estimate. Houthi forces used medium weapons and mortar shells in their attack.

Around 25 March 2025 (as reported), Houthi police forces and their supervisor aggressively assaulted an elderly man using the backs of their rifles in the district of Jabal Ras (coded to district center Al Marir) (Al Hudaydah) causing him serious head injuries and severe bleeding. The reason for the attack is unknown.

The reason for his abduction is suspected to be his Facebook posts criticizing the STC.

The attack severely injured the soldier affecting his liver, intestines and left leg causing him to be hospitalized in the ICU unit of the General Al-Thawra Hospital where he is in critical condition.

properties.assoc_actor_2: Al al Matari Tribal Group (Yemen)
properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Yemen Window

On 27 March 2025, an unidentified armed man shot and killed 1 young man as he was leaving As Saeed Mosque in Taizz governorate (coded to governorate capital Taizz). The reason behind the attack was due to dispute between the gunman and the man that took place earlier.

The individuals were taken to an unknown location. 7 abductees were reported in the district over 4 - 6 October and separately 8 abductees were reported over 5 - 7 October. It is unclear how many from the two claims are overlapping.

Around 8 January 2025 (week of), Houthi forces abducted around 20 civilians from Al Jaashi village and the surrounding villages in Ibb district (coded to district center Farad) (Ibb). The reason behind the abduction was due to the looting of a truck loaded with belongings of a Houthi leader near Al Jaashi village. They were taken to the security center prison run by the Houthi leader.
On 3 January 2025, a Houthi militant shot and killed 1 civilian in Yarim district (coded to district center Yarim) (Ibb). The reason behind the attack was not reported.
On 25 December 2024, Houthi forces shot and killed 1 civilian in As Sayyani district (coded to district center As Sayyani) (Ibb). The reason behind the incident was not reported.

The ship's security forces attempted to intercept the drone boat, but they were unsuccessful and it hit the ship, damaging it by puncturing a ballast tank. There were no casualties, and the ship processed to its next port of call. The Houthis claimed to have used 8 ballistic and cruise missiles, drones and drone boats in their attack. The ship was targeted earlier on that day (coded as a separate event).

properties.notes: On 10 November 2024, US forces conducted airstrikes targeting Houthi military camps and weapons storage facilities in Sanaa - Al Hafaa (As Sabain, Amanat al Asimah). Casualties unknown. This was the second attack on the same site after a previous airstrike targeted it on 9 November 2024 (coded as a separate event). CENTCOM claimed the targeting of multiple Houthi weapons storage facilities on 9-10 November without providing locations, while Houthi sources reported raids on several locations in Amanat al Asimah, Sanaa, and Amran governorates.

properties.assoc_actor_2: Military Forces of Yemen (2017-) Houthi

properties.notes: On 18 November 2024, an unidentified tribal gunman from Sadah governorate opened fire using a Kalashnikov at Houthi supervisor west of Sanaa city (coded to Sanaa - Qaryat Asr) (Maain, Amanat al Asimah). The Houthi official was critically injured and transferred to a hospital. The motive behind the attack is unknown, but one source speculated that it likely occurred due disagreements with other Houthi officials from Sadah Governorate over levies and land grabs in the Asr area west of Sanaa city.

On 10 January 2025, unidentified military forces (either Israeli or US) conducted strikes targeting Houthi military camps in Sanaa - Jabal an Nahdayn and Sanaa - Al Hafaa (As Sabain, Amanat al Asimah). Casualties unknown. Neither Israel nor the US claimed the strikes.

The airstrikes resulted in the killing of at least 4 people, likely Houthi militants, including the killing of 1 senior Houthi official, and the destruction of the base. 1 civilian child was also killed and at least 13 civilians (up to 15), including 5 children and 2 women, were injured. Sources differed on the identities of the victims as pro-Houthi sources claimed that the strikes targeted a residential building and killed at least 4 civilians, while anti-Houthi sources claimed that the strike targeted a building used by Houthi forces and only killed Houthi fighters. Total fatalities coded as at least 5.

Truman and nearby destroyers in the North Red Sea.

The beekeepers were in a vehicle looking for a location to set up their beehives when the landmine exploded. Sources differed as to the origin of the landmine (Houthi or anti-Houthi). Sources also reported conflicting numbers of fatalities between 2, 3, and 4. Fatalities coded as at least 2, following the most conservative estimate.

On 15 March 2025, Houthi police forces shot and severely injured 1 civilian at Al Hajf checkpoint in Al Udayn district (coded to district center Al Udayn) (Ibb). The reason behind the attack was not reported. The civilian was taken to the hospital.

properties.actor1: Military Forces of the United States (2025-)
properties.source: Al Arabiya; Al Hadath; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG
properties.notes: On 30 March 2025, US forces conducted at least 4 airstrikes targeting Houthi sites in Qaryat al Qabil (Bani Al Harith, Amanat al Asimah). The strikes targeted two launch platforms for the long-range Dhu Al Fiqar missiles. One source claimed the destroyed platforms were the last remaining Dhu Al Fiqar launch platforms. Casualties unknown.

properties.assoc_actor_2: Ash Sharani Tribal Group (Yemen)
properties.source: 2 December News; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen)
properties.notes: On 25 October 2024, armed men from Bayt ash Sharif tribe shot and killed a sheikh from the Ash Sharani tribe in Manzil ash Sharani (Hubaysh, Ibb). The civilian's sister was injured as she was with the civilian at the time of the shooting. The source claimed that the reason behind the attack was a land dispute.

properties.assoc_actor_2: Military Forces of Yemen (2017-) Houthi
properties.source: Al Masdar; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 1 April 2025, Ash Sharani tribal militia clashed with the Houthi-supported Bayt ash Sharif tribal militia in Manzil ash Sharani (Hubaysh, Ibb) after Houthi forces released suspects from the Bayt ash Sharif tribal militia who were sentenced for the killing of an individual from the Ash Sharani tribe in October 2024. Houthi forces raided Manzil ash Sharani in support of Bayt ash Sahrif and clashed with the Ash Sharani tribesmen. The clashes caused 2 injuries and 4 fatalities, including 1 injury and at least 2 fatalities on the side of the Ash Sharani tribal militia in addition to 1 injury and at least 1 fatality on the side of the Bayt ash Sharif tribal militia. Total fatalities coded as at least 4.

On 3 October 2024, Giants Brigades clashed with tribesmen from Hammam tribe near the solar energy project in Ataq district (coded to district center Ataq) (Shabwah) injuring 1 member of the Giants Brigades and 1 other from Hammam tribe. The clashes erupted after members of the Giants Brigades dressed in civilian clothes, attempted to detain members of the Hamam tribe for inspecting plans of one of the disputed land.

properties.assoc_actor_2: National Resistance (Guards of the Republic)
properties.source: Al Masdar; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Mareb Press; Undisclosed Source
properties.notes: On 1 October 2024, for the second consecutive day, clashes continued between Al Ulqimah tribal militants and National Resistance Forces (coded under WCJF) in Al Dharifa (Al Waziiyah, Taizz). The clashes caused an unspecified number of injuries. The fighting involved firearms, heavy weapons, and drones, with the National Resistance Forces establishing a base in a school and a market and Al Ulqimah tribal gunmen positioned on Jabal Naman. The exchange of gunfire began when NRF set up a security point the day prior. On 5 October, the governor of Taizz mediated an agreement between the clashing sides to cease clashes and withdraw armed groups and military forces from the area.

properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Al Masirah; Ansar Allah; Tradewinds; UKMTO; Undisclosed Source; Yemen News Agency (SABA) - Houthi; Yemeni Army - Houthi
properties.notes: On 1 October 2024, at an unspecified time, Houthi forces claimed a cruise missile attack targeting the Marathopolis container ship (IMO: 9477804) in the Indian Ocean (coded to Northwestern Indian Ocean), claiming affiliation with companies in violation of the Houthi ban to call at Israeli ports. There were no fatalities or injuries as the missile likely impacted the water before reaching the intended target. The attack was not confirmed by other sources, and the ship was laden off Oman the following day before heading east to the next port of call in India. The Houthis claimed to target the ship again on the same day (coded separately). The Houthis have claimed a previous attack in the Marathopolis around 9 July that was also not confirmed by other sources (coded separately).

properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Al Masirah; Ansar Allah; Tradewinds; UKMTO; UN Panel of Experts; Undisclosed Source; Yemen News Agency (SABA) - Houthi; Yemeni Army - Houthi
properties.notes: On 1 October 2024, at around between 4:50 and 5:55 am Sanaa time, Houthi forces fired 4 missiles targeting the Cordelia Moon crude oil tanker (IMO: 9297888) in the South Red Sea around 64 nm northwest of Al Hudaydah, claiming affiliation with companies in violation of the Houthi ban to call at Israeli ports. The missiles failed to hit the ship and splashed in the water close to it, with the last missile hitting around 100 m from the ship. There were no casualties or damages. The Houthis claimed to have used 8 ballistic and cruise missiles, drones and drone boats in their attack. The ship was targeted again later on that day (coded as a separate event).

properties.source: Tradewinds; UKMTO; UN Panel of Experts; Undisclosed Source
properties.notes: On 1 October 2024, at around 7:15 am Sanaa time, suspected Houthi forces fired 1 missile targeting the Minoan Courage bulk carrier (IMO: 9288526) in the South Red Sea around 97 nm northwest of Al Hudaydah. The missile hit the ship, damaging its engine, and causing a fire, but causing no casualties among crew. The ship was stationary at the time of the attack. According to JMIC, the attack was likely unintentional, as the Houthis were targeting the Cordelia Moon crude oil tanker nearby (coded as a separate event). The Houthis did not claim the attack.

properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Al Masirah; Ansar Allah; Tradewinds; UKMTO; Undisclosed Source; Yemen News Agency (SABA) - Houthi; Yemeni Army - Houthi
properties.notes: On 1 October 2024, at an unspecified time, Houthi forces claimed a drone attack targeting the Marathopolis container ship (IMO: 9477804) in the Arabian sea (coded to West Arabian Sea) northeast of the Socotra archipelago, claiming affiliation with companies in violation of the Houthi ban to call at Israeli ports. There were no fatalities or injuries as the drone likely impacted the water before reaching the intended target. The attack was not confirmed by other sources, and the ship was laden off Oman the following day before heading east to the next port of call in India. The Houthis claimed to target the ship a previous time on the same day (coded separately). The Houthis have claimed a previous attack in the Marathopolis around 9 July that was also not confirmed by other sources (coded separately).

On 6 October 2024, Houthi snipers shot 1 civilian as he was leaving his home in Taizz - Usayfarah (Al Qahirah, Taizz), injuring him in the leg. The reason behind the attack was not reported. There were no fatalities.

properties.actor1: AQAP: Al Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula
properties.actor2: Military Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council - STC Shabwani Defense Forces
properties.notes: On 7 October 2024, suspected AQAP militants clashed with STC 1st Shabwani Defense Brigades forces in Al Musayniah (As Said, Shabwah). SDF thwarted the attack and caused 'heavy AQAP losses'. Unclear fatalities coded as 10.

properties.actor1: Military Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council - 22nd Armored Brigade
properties.source: Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Undisclosed Source; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 7 October 2024, fighters from the IRG Islah-affiliated 22nd Armored Brigade abducted 1 civilian from his home in Taizz - Al Mujliyah (Al Qahirah, Taizz). He was taken to an unknown location. The abduction followed a previous incident on 27 September 2024, when the fighters destroyed the fence of the civilian's home and assaulted the family members who intervened (coded as a separate incident).

properties.assoc_actor_1: Police Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council
properties.notes: On 8 October 2024, clashes erupted between tribesmen from Az Zaghruri and Al Mukhalifah tribes over a disputed plot of land near Al Hadhrami restaurant in Taizz - Bir Bashah (Al Mudhaffar, Taizz). The clash, which resulted in 1 injury, began when Al Mukhalifah tribesmen attempted to seize the land with state security support (coded as police), despite a Az Zaghruri family's valid lease.

properties.source: Reuters; Undisclosed Source; Yemeni Army - Houthi
properties.notes: On 10 October 2024, at an unspecified time, Houthi forces claimed a cruise missile attack targeting the St. John container ship (IMO: 9634646) in the Northwestern Indian Ocean, claiming affiliation with companies in violation of the Houthi ban to call at Israeli ports. There were no fatalities or injuries as the missile likely impacted the water before reaching the intended target. The attack was not confirmed by other sources. Houthi forces targeted the ship possibly because of its affiliation with Greece-based company Eastern Mediterranean Maritime, which had other ships affiliated to it dock at Israeli ports in early May. The St. John was located off the coast of Somalia, en route to the UAE.

properties.source: AP; Tradewinds; UK Royal Navy; UKMTO; UN Panel of Experts; Undisclosed Source; Yemeni Army - Houthi
properties.notes: On 10 October 2024, at around 3:10 am Sanaa time, Houthi forces fired 1 projectile (likely a drone according to Maritime intelligence firm Diaplous) targeting the Olympic Spirit chemical/oil products tanker (IMO: 9327097) in the South Red Sea around 70 nm southwest of Al Hudaydah, claiming US affiliation of the ship. The projectile hit the ship's bridge, causing minor damage, but no casualties. Houthi forces claimed to have used 11 ballistic missiles and 2 drones in their attack against the Olympic Spirit. The ship was targeted twice more on that day (coded as separate events). The US affiliation of the ship was not verified. According to TradeWinds, the ship was managed by the Greece-based Dynamic Shipmanagement at the time of the attack, although it used to be managed by the US-based Oaktree Capital Management and is still registered under the latter in at least one shipping database.

properties.source: AP; Tradewinds; UK Royal Navy; UKMTO; UN Panel of Experts; Undisclosed Source; Yemeni Army - Houthi
properties.notes: On 10 October 2024, at around 6:30 am Sanaa time, Houthi forces fired 2 ballistic missiles targeting the Olympic Spirit chemical/oil products tanker (IMO: 9327097) in the South Red Sea around 70 nm southwest of Al Hudaydah, claiming US affiliation of the ship. There were no fatalities or injuries as the missiles impacted the water in close proximity to the ship. Houthi forces claimed to have used 11 ballistic missiles and 2 drones in their attack. The ship was targeted twice more on that day (coded as separate events). The US affiliation of the ship was not verified. According to TradeWinds, the ship was managed by the Greece-based Dynamic Shipmanagement at the time of the attack, although it used to be managed by the US-based Oaktree Capital Management and is still registered under the latter in at least one shipping database.

properties.source: AP; Tradewinds; UK Royal Navy; UKMTO; UN Panel of Experts; Undisclosed Source; Yemeni Army - Houthi
properties.notes: On 10 October 2024, at around 9:20 am Sanaa time, Houthi forces fired 1 ballistic missile targeting the Olympic Spirit chemical/oil products tanker (IMO: 9327097) in the South Red Sea around 70 nm southwest of Al Hudaydah, claiming US affiliation of the ship. The projectile failed to reach the ship and impacted the waters in close proximity to the ship. Houthi forces claimed to have used 11 ballistic missiles and 2 drones in their attack. The ship was targeted twice more on that day (coded as separate events). The US affiliation of the ship was not verified. According to TradeWinds, the ship was managed by the Greece-based Dynamic Shipmanagement at the time of the attack, although it used to be managed by the US-based Oaktree Capital Management and is still registered under the latter in at least one shipping database.

properties.notes: On 11 November 2024, suspected US forces conducted 2 airstrikes targeting Houthi sites in Al Rahba area in As Safra district (coded to district center Ash Shawati) (Sadah). Casualties unknown. US forces did not claim the attacks.

properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Al Masirah; Ansar Allah; AP; BBC News; Mareb Press; Undisclosed Source; US Naval Institute; Yemen News Agency (SABA) - Houthi; Yemen Window; Yemeni Army - Houthi
properties.notes: On 11 November 2024, at an unspecified time, Houthi forces claimed to have fired an unspecified number of drones and cruise missiles targeting the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier in the Arabian Sea (coded to West Arabian Sea), claiming they targeted US forces while they were preparing to strike targets in Yemen. The Houthis claimed that the attack lasted 8 hours and was 'successful', while US authorities stated that they were unaware of any attack targeting the aircraft carrier. The Houthis claimed a separate attack targeting two other US ships in the Strait of Bab el Mandab on the same day (coded separately).

properties.actor1: Military Forces of the United States (2021-2025)
properties.source: 2 December News; Al Masdar; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Mareb Press; Undisclosed Source; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 12 November 2024, suspected US forces carried drone attacks targeting Houthi mobile rocket launchers in the Amsmakhiya area As Sawmaah district (coded to district center As Sawmaah) and an airport in Dhi Naim district (coded to district center Dhi Naim) (Al Bayda). Sources differed on reported fatalities. Some sources claimed Houthi elements left the mobile launcher before it was targeted and none were killed, while others claimed at least 10 Houthi soldiers, including a leader, were killed in the strikes. Some sources also claimed AQAP elements were killed along with Houthi fighters, but this is unconfirmed. Fatalities coded as 0 following the most conservative estimate. US forces did not claim the attacks.

The clashes also resulted in the destruction of 1 Houthi sniper position and 1 STC fortification. Fatalities coded as 5 and split across 3 events. 2 fatalities coded to this event.

properties.source: Aden al Ghad; Al Masdar; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Al Masirah; Ansar Allah; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); The Washington Institute; Tradewinds; UKMTO; UN Panel of Experts; Undisclosed Source; Yemen News Agency (SABA) - Houthi; Yemeni Army - Houthi
properties.notes: On 18 November 2024, at around 12:58 pm Sanaa time, Houthi forces fired at least 1 ballistic missile targeting the Anadolu S bulk carrier (IMO: 9667758) in the Gulf of Aden around 60 nm southeast of Aden, claiming affiliation with companies in violation of the Houthi ban to call at Israeli ports. There were no fatalities or injuries as the missile impacted the water around the ship. The ship continued to its next port of call. According to JMIC, the ship had an 'indirect association' to Israel. The vessel's owner was linked to at least one ship seen in Israel in early October, but it was unclear if it had more direct ties to Israel. The ship was targeted once before the previous day (coded as a separate event).

properties.source: The Washington Institute; Tradewinds; UKMTO; UN Panel of Experts; Undisclosed Source; Yemeni Army - Houthi
properties.notes: On 17 November 2024, at around 7:13 pm Sanaa time, suspected Houthi forces fired at least 1 ballistic missile targeting the Anadolu S bulk carrier (IMO: 9667758) in the Strait of Bab el Mandeb around 25 nm west of Al Mukha, claiming affiliation with companies in violation of the Houthi ban to call at Israeli ports. There were no fatalities or injuries as the missile impacted the water 500m from the ship. According to JMIC, the ship had an 'indirect association' with Israel. The vessel's owner was linked to at least one ship seen in Israel in early October, but it was unclear if it had more direct ties to Israel. The ship continued to its next port of call. The ship was targeted again the following day (coded as a separate event).

The source claimed the prisoner was poisoned through her food which worsened her medial status in addition to the torture she had been exposed to during her detention. properties.notes: On 10 January 2025, US forces and Israeli fighter jets coincidentally conducted three waves of airstrikes targeting Houthi sites and Yemeni infrastructure, including 6 airstrikes targeting the Al Hudaydah - Port (Al Hali, Al Hudaydah) and 7 airstrikes targeting the port of Ras Isa (As Salif, Al Hudaydah), killing 1 and injuring three workers. Fatalities split across 2 events. 0 Fatality coded to this event. At the Ras Isa port, three oil tanks were targeted, two of which were empty. The third tank exploded, causing a fire on an oil tanker docked at the port and damaging a pumping pipe from another tanker. The strikes also targeted four moorings in the port and a marine launch tugboat. Houthi gunfire also damaged a third ship in the port.

properties.notes: On 10 January 2025, Houthi forces conducted a drone strike targeting IRG forces, including Nation Shield Forces, in Aqabat al Halhal front (Lawdar, Abyan) resulting in the injury of 1 IRG soldier. Houthi forces also clashed with Southern Resistance forces, the 115th Infantry Brigade (coded as IRG generic forces), and the 2nd Presidential Protection Brigade forces along the fronts of Aqabat al Halhal and Aqabat Thirah (Lawdar, Abyan). Clashes took place on 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 11 January 2025. 1 Southern Resistance soldier was injured. Between January 9-11, Houthi drones were seen flying over the front.

properties.source: 2 December News; Al Harf 28; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Al Sahil Al Gharbi; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Undisclosed Source; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 3 January 2025, Houthi forces opened live fire on the car of a member of the Dhu Husayn tribe at the Zolaq checkpoint in Al Yatimah (Khabb wa Ash Shaaf, Al Jawf). The reason behind the attack was not reported. Militants from the Dhu Husayn tribe responded by attacking the Houthi forces with medium and heavy weapons and burning 1 vehicle belonging to the Houthi in the same area. Casualties unknown.

properties.actor2: Military Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council
properties.source: 26 September News - IRG; Al Harf 28; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Al Sahil Al Gharbi; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Undisclosed Source; Yemen Window; Yemeni Army - IRG
properties.notes: On 4 January 2025, Houthi forces clashed with IRG forces in southern Marib (coded to Jabal al Balaq ash Sharqi front) (Marib). Casualties unknown.

properties.actor2: Military Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council
properties.source: 2 December News; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Undisclosed Source
properties.notes: On 4 January 2025, Houthi artillery forces shelled IRG forces on the air defense base front in Taizz - Bir Bashah (Al Mudhaffar, Taizz). IRG forces responded with unspecified means. The shelling took place on 25 - 29 December 2024 and 4 January 2025 resulting in the killing of 1 IRG soldier. 1 fatality split between 6 events. 0 fatality coded to this event.

properties.actor2: Military Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council - STC Hazm Brigades
properties.source: Al Sahil Al Gharbi; Dera Al Ganoob; Mareb Press; Undisclosed Source
properties.notes: On 7 January 2025, Houthi forces attacked positions of the 4th STC Hazm Brigades and clashed using artillery, medium and light weapons in Wadi Al Haseemah in Uzlat Al Abus region (Hayfan, Taizz), killing 3 Houthi fighters and injuring at least 1 STC soldier.

properties.assoc_actor_2: Labor Group (Yemen)
properties.source: Al Harf 28; Al Jazeera; Al Masdar; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Israeli Air Force; Times of Israel; Undisclosed Source; Yemen News Agency (SABA) - Houthi
properties.notes: On 10 January 2025, Israeli fighter jets conducted three waves of airstrikes targeting Houthi sites and Yemeni infrastructure, including 13 airstrikes targeting the Hizyaz Power station in Hizyaz (Sanhan, Sanaa), injuring 3 workers and damaging a number of houses in the surrounding area.

properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Twitter; Undisclosed Source
properties.notes: On 7 January 2025, clashes between Al Bahaj and Al Muhayqin tribes erupted over a land boundary dispute in Merkhah Al Ulya district (coded to district center Al Aqir) (Shabwah), injuring at least 5 people. A tribal mediation later established an eight-day truce.

properties.source: Al Masdar; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Undisclosed Source
properties.notes: On 8 January 2025, clashes erupted between Houthi forces and Al Masud tribesmen in Hanakat Al Masud (Khubzah) (Al Quraishyah, Al Bayda). The clashes came after a siege that Houthi forces had put on the village for few days (coded separately). The clashes resulted in human losses on both sides. Unclear fatalities coded as 3. The reason behind the clashes was that Houthi forces searching for wanted people involving in the killing of a Houthi supervisor (mushrif).

properties.source: 2 December News; Aden al Ghad; Al Harf 28; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Sam Organization; Undisclosed Source
properties.notes: On 9 January 2025, for second consecutive day, clashes erupted between Houthi forces and Al Masud tribesmen in Hanakat Al Masud (Khubzah) (Al Quraishyah, Al Bayda). Houthi forces used heavy weapons, tanks, and drones targeting residential areas in the village resulting in the killing of 2 civilians and the injury of 11 others, including 3 women, while a number of Houthi fighters were killed during the clashes. The tribesmen destroyed 3 Houthi military vehicles and 2 drones, while Houthi attacks damaged a mosque and a number of houses, and destroyed 1 house completely. Unspecified fatalities coded as 3 on the Houthi side. Total fatalities coded as at least 5.

properties.assoc_actor_2: al-Qayfa Tribal Militia (Yemen)
properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Undisclosed Source; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 10 January 2025, for third consecutive day, clashes erupted between Houthi forces and Al Masud tribesmen in Hanakat Al Masud (Khubzah) (Al Quraishyah, Al Bayda), on the same day the clashes expanded to nearby areas and between Houthi forces and Qayfa tribesmen in As Sabalah and Al Qaryah as Sawda. Houthi forces used heavy weapons, tanks, and drones targeting residential areas in the village, resulting in the killing and injury of a number of civilians and a number of Houthi fighters, including a senior officer who was the Director of Criminal Investigations. The tribesmen destroyed 3 Houthi military vehicles and 2 checkpoints. Houthi forces claimed that the campaign targeted Islamic State militants in the village and that 1 IS militant was killed due to the explosion of a suicide belt he was wearing during clashes with Houthi forces. Unclear fatalities on the Houthi side coded as 3. Unclear fatalities coded as 10 on the civilian side. Total fatalities coded as 13 split between 3 events. 5 fatalities coded to this event.

properties.actor2: Military Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council - STC Support and Reinforcement Brigades
properties.assoc_actor_2: Military Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council - STC Saiqa Brigades
properties.notes: On 10 January 2025, Houthi forces clashed with joint forces of the STC-affiliated 5th Support and Reinforcement Brigade and 13th Saiqa Brigade along the Hamala Hawamrah front (coded to Jabal Humalah) (Al Qabbaytah, Lahij). Clashes took place on 10 and 12 January 2025. 5 Houthi fighters were killed, and 4 were injured. 3 STC-affiliated soldiers were wounded. 5 fatalities coded across 2 events. 3 fatalities coded to this event.

properties.source: Al Harf 28; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Undisclosed Source; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 4 January 2025, unknown militants planted and detonated an explosive device inside a steel and iron factory in Al Mukalla City (Hadramawt), rendering the facility non-operational. There were no casualties.

properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Undisclosed Source; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 2 January 2025, Houthi forces fired a ballistic missile at the house of a tribal leader in Sawlan village in the northern part of Murays area (coded to Jabal Murays) (Qaatabah, Ad Dali), destroying the house. There were no casualties.

properties.actor1: AQAP: Al Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula
properties.actor2: Military Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council
properties.source: Aden al Ghad; Al Harf 28; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Mareb Press; Undisclosed Source
properties.notes: On 19 December 2024, AQAP militants clashed with soldiers of the 5th Infantry Brigade in an ambush in Husn Al Saeed area near Ahwar district (coded to district center Ahwar) (Abyan). The clashes resulted in the killing of 4 IRG soldiers while 3 soldiers were taken captive. The soldiers were part of a military committee transporting the salaries of the 5th Infantry Brigade in a military vehicles when they were ambushed. The salaries, an amount of around 300 thousand Saudi Rials, the soldiers' weapons, and the military vehicles were looted.

properties.actor1: AQAP: Al Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula

properties.source: An Nusra Media; Dera Al Ganoob; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Undisclosed Source
properties.notes: On 17 December 2024, STC forces repelled an attack by AQAP forces around the eastern part of Mudiyah district (coded to Imbaqirah based on recent activity) (Abyan). AQAP claimed they targeted STC forces with a drone dropping shells. The clash resulted in a 'number of losses' on the AQAP side, while injuring 2 STC soldiers. Unclear fatalities coded as 3 on the AQAP side.

properties.source: Akhbar Taiz; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Undisclosed Source; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 18 December 2024, Houthi sniper shot and injured 1 civilian in Al Hanini village in Al Quhayfah (Maqbanah, Taizz).

properties.source: Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Undisclosed Source; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 19 December 2024, Houthi forces abducted 1 Doctor/Hospital manager in Al Qiadah Street in Shuaub district (coded to district center Sanaa - Shuaub) (Amanat al Asimah). The reason behind the abduction was a tweet he published on his Twitter account supporting the departure of Bashar Al Assad from Syria. He was taken to an unknown destination.

The strikes resulted in the killing of 7 workers in the port of As-Salif, and 2 others in the port of the Ras Issa oil facility, and injuring 3 others across all locations. 0 fatality coded to this location.

properties.actor1: Military Forces of the United States (2021-2025)

properties.notes: On 21 December 2024, overnight, US forces conducted airstrikes targeting Houthi military sites and weapons storage facilities in Sanaa - Al Hafaa (As Sabain, Amanat al Asimah). Casualties unknown. CENTCOM claimed the targeting of a Houthi missile storage facility and a command-and-control facility on 21 December without providing locations, while local Houthi and non-Houthi sources reported raids on several locations in Amanat al Asimah and Sanaa governorates.

CENTCOM claimed the targeting of a Houthi missile storage facility and a command-and-control facility on 21 December without providing locations, while local Houthi and non-Houthi sources reported raids on several locations in Amanat al Asimah and Sanaa governorates.

Casualties unknown. CENTCOM claimed the targeting of a Houthi missile storage facility and a command-and-control facility on 21 December without providing locations, while local Houthi and non-Houthi sources reported raids on several locations in Amanat al Asimah and Sanaa governorates.

properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Al Sahil Al Gharbi; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Undisclosed Source; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 22 December 2024, a tribal leader opened fire at Houthi fighters who raided his home and assaulted one of his sons in Al Ghurairah village of Wald Rabi district (coded to district center Hammat Sarar) (Al Bayda), killing 1 Houthi soldier. Houthi forces besieged the village and arbitrarily arrested residents after the clash.

properties.source: 2 December News; Aden al Ghad; Al Harf 28; Al Sahil Al Gharbi; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Undisclosed Source
properties.notes: On 23 December 2024, Houthi forces shelled residential homes in Mamal al Bumiyah (Maqbanah, Taizz) killing 2 children, and injuring 2 other children. Those injured were transferred to Hays hospital for treatment.

properties.source: Aden al Ghad; Al Jazeera; Al Masdar; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); UN Panel of Experts; Undisclosed Source; Yemen News Agency (SABA) - Houthi

The strikes on Al Hudaydah - Port resulted in the killing of 3 civilians and the injury of 10 others.

properties.assoc_actor_2: UN: United Nations
properties.source: Aden al Ghad; Al Jazeera; Al Masdar; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; France24; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); UN Panel of Experts; Undisclosed Source; Yemen News Agency (SABA) - Houthi
properties.notes: On 26 December 2024, Israeli forces conducted 7 airstrikes targeting the control tower, waiting hall, and the runway of Sanaa - Sanaa International Airport (Bani Al Harith, Amanat al Asimah) and Hezyaz power station (coded separately to Hizyaz) (Sanhan, Sanaa). The strikes on Sanaa International Airport resulted in the killing of 3 civilians and the injury of 30 others. A UN humanitarian pilot was among the injured in the strike targeting the airport.

properties.actor2: Military Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council
properties.notes: On 26 December 2024, Houthi forces shelled IRG forces in the air defense base front in Taizz - Bir Bashah (Al Mudhaffar, Taizz). The shelling took place on 25 - 29 December 2024 and 4 January 2025 resulting in the killing of 1 IRG soldier. 1 fatality split between 6 events. 1 fatality coded to this event.

properties.source: Maritime Executive; Undisclosed Source; Yemeni Army - Houthi
properties.notes: On 27 December 2024, at an unspecified time, Houthi forces claimed to have fired a number of drones targeting the Santa Ursula container ship (IMO: 9430387) near Socotra in the Arabian sea (coded to West Arabian Sea), claiming affiliation with companies in violation of the Houthi ban to call at Israeli ports. There were no fatalities or injuries as the drones likely impacted the water before reaching the intended target. The attack was not confirmed by other sources. The ship was bound to Salalah in Oman and belonged to Maersk shipping company, who denied the attack took place, and whose ships have been attacked by the Houthis previously.

On 26 December 2024, Houthi forces abducted 1 civilian from his house in Damt district (coded to district center Hammam Damt) (Ad Dali). The reason behind the abduction was to pressure his brother who defected from the Houthi group in the middle of last year.

properties.source: 26 September News - IRG; Al Sahil Al Gharbi; Undisclosed Source
properties.notes: On 27 December 2024, Houthi forces fired mortar shells at Taizz - Wadi ad Dahi (Al Mudhaffar, Taizz), injuring 1 child. There were no fatalities.

properties.actor2: AQAP: Al Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula
properties.source: 2 December News; Al Harf 28; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Undisclosed Source; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 26 February 2025, STC Security Belt forces launched a security campaign targeting AQAP positions and hideouts in the Wadi Awmran, Wadi ar Rafd, and Wadi Matar regions, Al Hanakah (Mudiyah), and Wadi Al Khatmah and Ar Rubayz areas in Mudiyah district (coded to district center Mudiyah) (Abyan). IRG drones were used in Wadi ar Rafd, which targeted and killed a senior AQAP leader. Overall, The operation resulted in the STC taking over a number of AQAP bases and camps, and the killing of 3 AQAP militants and 1 STC soldier and the injury of several AQAP fighters and at least 4 STC soldiers. STC forces also seized AQAP weapons stockpiles, and caused remaining AQAP fighters to flee towards Al Bayda governorate. 4 fatalities split across 5 events. 1 fatality coded to this location.

properties.assoc_actor_1: Police Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council
properties.source: Aden al Ghad; Akhbar Taiz; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Undisclosed Source; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 23 February 2025, clashes erupted between unidentified armed men and a qat seller at Al Qubbah qat market in Taizz city (Taizz) resulting in the killing of 1 civilian and the injury of others. The clashes broke out as an old man wanted to return the qat he bought from the seller, and when the seller refused a verbal dispute happened. The security forces intervened and asked the old man to leave the market, but he raised his weapon at them and the security shot and injured him in his thighs. Hours later, the old man's sons came to the market, opened fire, and clashed with the qat seller. The clash resulted in the fatality of 2 people and the injury of 5 others.

properties.source: 2 December News; Aden al Ghad; Al Harf 28; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Twitter; Undisclosed Source
properties.notes: On 4 March 2025, at dawn, a tribal clash erupted between Al Sharman and Al Marsas tribes in Wadi Dara (Nisab, Shabwah), over a longstanding blood feud. The fighting resulted in 1 fatality and 2 injuries.

properties.notes: On 3 March 2025, a suspected Houthi remnant munition exploded in Al Kirmum area (Nisab, Shabwah), killing 2 children while they were herding animals. The victims, aged 15 and 13, found the unexploded ordnance near their home and were handling it when it detonated.

properties.actor1: Military Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council - Military Police

properties.notes: On 2 March 2025, IRG Military police forces stationed at a military checkpoint under the Taizz Military Axis opened fire on civilians from the Muhammashin ethnic group in Al Madina Al Sakaniya in Taizz city (Taizz), injuring 4 people, including a woman. The shooting occurred following a dispute among civilians during Iftar meal distribution, after which some individuals called for military intervention from the soldiers who were in a nearby checkpoint. Upon arrival, the soldiers fired randomly without prior. The incident led to a protest outside the governorate building, demanding an investigation and accountability (coded as a separate event).

properties.assoc_actor_2: Dhu Ghailan Tribal Group (Yemen); Prisoners (Yemen)
properties.source: Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Al Masirah; Twitter; Undisclosed Source; Yemen News Agency (SABA) - Houthi
properties.notes: Around 3 March 2025 (as reported), a prisoner from Dhu Ghailan tribe was tortured to death in Marib city (Marib). His body with signs of torture in a street in the city on 3 March. Sources differ on whether he was previously held in a Houthi or IRG-run detention facility. The individual had been detained for three months, with family members allegedly asked to provide guarantees for his release. No official explanation has been issued regarding the circumstances of his death. Other source claim the killing was a Houthi targeting the Dhu Ghailan tribe.

The clash came as part of a military campaign against smugglers. The following day tribesmen abducted the brother-in-law of a Giants Brigades leader in retaliation (coded separately).

The shelling came after IRG forces extended the opening hours of the eastern access of the city to 24 hours. The access connects Taizz city and Al Hawban. Casualties unknown.

properties.source: Al Harf 28; Al Masdar; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Mareb Press; Undisclosed Source; Yemen Window

The confrontation escalated when security forces deployed to mediate came under fire, leading to fighting with light and medium weapons. The clashes resulted in the deaths of 2 IRG soldiers and at least 1 tribesman (some sources reported 1 IRG fatalities and several fatalities among the tribesmen instead), injuries on both sides, and the destruction of 3 military vehicles. At least 3 fatalities in total.

The reason behind the clashes was that the leader of the 4th Brigade of 2nd division of the Nation's Shield forces along with at least 25 soldiers (some sources reported 70) attempted to smuggle all the brigade's equipment and weapons from the Qawa military camp. The leaders of the 1st and 4th brigades of 1st division of the IRG Nation's Shield forces pursued him, clashed with him, and eventually arrested him. The clashes resulted in the killing of 1 and the injury of 3 soldiers from the 1st division, and the injury of 6 soldiers from the 4th brigade of the 2nd division.

The US did not claim the strikes.

One source reported that US airstrikes in Sadah (presumably referring to this event) injured the commander of the 6th Military Region, who reportedly succumbed to his wounds and died on 2 November 2024, along with four Iranian experts who were injured. properties.actor1: Police Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council - STC Security Belt Forces
properties.assoc_actor_1: Military Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council - STC Support and Reinforcement Brigades
properties.actor2: AQAP: Al Qaeda in the Arabian Peninsula
properties.source: 2 December News; Al Harf 28; Al Mashhad Al Yemeni - Pro-IRG; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Undisclosed Source; Yemen Window
properties.notes: On 26 February 2025, STC Security Belt forces launched a security campaign targeting AQAP positions and hideouts in the Wadi Awmran, Wadi ar Rafd, and Wadi Matar regions, Al Hanakah (Mudiyah), and Wadi Al Khatmah and Ar Rubayz areas in Mudiyah district (coded to district center Mudiyah) (Abyan). IRG drones were used in Wadi ar Rafd, which targeted and killed a senior AQAP leader. Overall, The operation resulted in the STC taking over a number of AQAP bases and camps, and the killing of 3 AQAP militants and 1 STC soldier and the injury of several AQAP fighters and at least 4 STC soldiers. STC forces also seized AQAP weapons stockpiles, and caused remaining AQAP fighters to flee towards Al Bayda governorate. 4 fatalities split across 5 events. 1 fatality coded to this location.

properties.assoc_actor_2: Prisoners (Yemen)

He was released on October suffering severe physical and psychological damage. Sources reported that torture took place 10 months prior to the date of release.

properties.actor2: Military Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council - STC Hazm Brigades
properties.source: Dera Al Ganoob; Khabar News Agency (Yemen); Mareb Press
properties.notes: On 31 March 2025, STC Hazm Brigades thwarted an attack by Houthi forces leading to clashes in which medium and heavy weapons were used, on the Kirsh front (coded to Shurayjah) and the Shifan front (coded to Wadi as Sahi) (Al Qabbaytah, Lahij). The clashes caused 3 fatalities and 1 injury on the side of the STC Hazm Brigades and 'heavy losses' among Houthi forces. Unclear fatalities coded as 10 on the Houthi side. Total fatalities coded as 13 and split between two locations.

properties.actor1: Police Forces of Yemen (2022-2026) Presidential Leadership Council - STC Aden Security
properties.notes: Around 15 January 2025 (month of), in the evening, STC police forces (coded as STC Aden Security) abducted 1 civilian from his house in Al Mualla district (coded to district center Aden - Al Maalla) (Aden) and took him to Wadah Hall Prison. After the abduction and severe beating, the forces took him to his house and looted his family's money, gold, and car. The reason behind the abduction was not reported. He was released on October suffering severe physical and psychological damage.

properties.assoc_actor_2: Unidentified Tribal Group (Yemen)
properties.notes: Around 15 October 2024 (month of), Houthi forces abducted 1 tribal sheikh in Hadiyah (Maqbanah, Taizz) after raiding the village. He was taken to an unknown destination. The reason behind the abduction was not reported.

On 3 March 2025, Houthi forces attacked (severity unclear) and abducted 1 poet from a market in Dhamar city (Dhamar City, Dhamar). The reason behind the abduction was the poet's opinions and public criticism of human rights violations expressed in his Facebook posts. He was taken to the city's police station, then transferred him to the Criminal Investigation Department prison.

properties.source: Yemeni Network for Rights and Freedoms
properties.notes: On 17 January 2025, Houthi sniper shot and killed 1 woman in Ad Durayhimi district (coded to district center Ad Durayhimi) (Al Hudaydah) and injured 1 other civilian in his leg. One hour later, Houthi sniper shot and injured 1 other civilian near his house in the district.

properties.assoc_actor_2: Labor Group (Yemen)
properties.source: Yemeni Network for Rights and Freedoms
properties.notes: On 5 February 2025, Houthi forces raided a house and arbitrarily arrested 1 civilian, who was a public servant, in Al Hali district (coded to district center Al Hudaydah - Al Hali) (Al Hudaydah). The reason behind the abduction was not reported. He was taken to an unknown destination.

properties.assoc_actor_2: Teachers (Yemen)
properties.source: Yemeni Network for Rights and Freedoms
properties.notes: On 5 March 2025, at dawn, Houthi forces raided a house and abducted 1 teacher in Al Hudaydah governorate (coded to governorate capital Al Hudaydah). He was taken to an unknown destination. The reason behind the abduction was not reported.
